calendar.matters:
Petition For Termination Of Irrevocable Trust On Consent Of All Beneficiaries Or Due To Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Jun-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Termination Of Irrevocable Trust On Consent Of All Beneficiaries Or Due To Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Jun-03-2019 Continued To Jun-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nathaniel Bigger for Lidia Ramirez

Mr. Bigger stated there is a cap of $2,000.00 of the Church Street property. Mr. Bigger further stated that his client was given dogs in Trust, Wells Fargo owned 1099 property, his client had to vacate the property with the dogs, and his client has been accruing tremendous costs living in tough conditions in Oakland. The Court granted The Petition for Order Appointing Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Joseph. Not reported.

Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-05-2018 Continued To Jun-12-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Continued To Jun-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-17-2018 Continued To May-22-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-09-2018 Continued To Apr-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Continued To Apr-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nathaniel Bigger for and with Lidia G. Ramirez (Petitioner)
Mark Walter in Propria Persona (Proposed Trustee)
Vern Adkins for SF SPCA

Mr. Adkins stated he withdraws his objections to the petition. Mr. Bigger stated he has a signed settlement regarding the only asset effecting SF SPCA. Under the settlement, the proposed successor trustee is to receive a direct payment of $2,000 in addition to the scheduled 1% fee for distribution of the asset to which the SF SPCA has the remainder interest. The Court stated other beneficiaries should be aware of this settlement and inquired further about extraordinary fees. The Court added there needed to be an amendment of the petition with proper notice. Mr. Bigger replied that he can have an amendment on file within a couple of days. The Petition for Order Appointing Successor Trustee is continued to April 3, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Shirley Le. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Continued To Mar-06-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nathaniel Bigger, for and with petitioner Lidia Ramirez; Vern Adkins for SF SPCA; Mark Walther, proposed Trustee.

The Court asked whether objector SF SPCA planned to seek the appointment of a different trustee. Mr. Walther stated that his client did not have an alternative trustee in mind, but that it was concerned about the fee arrangements with the proposed trustee, and that he thought an acceptable fee arrangement could be negotiated. He suggested a brief continuance of the hearing so the matter could be discussed further. Mr. Bigger said his preference was for the appointment of a successor trustee as soon as possible. The Court noted that changing the terms would necessitate an amendment and notice. The matter was continued to March 6, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)
